content: Just as an Intrinsic Curiosity Module rewards an autonomous reinforcement learning agent for exploring unfamiliar states to improve generalization, we can reframe the general definition of an agent itself as a system that encodes its own intrinsic curiosity signal as a core identity property, rather than treating curiosity as an add-on module. This means any adaptive agent (not just AI reinforcement learning agents) inherently sustains its agency by prioritizing exploration of uncertain unvisited states over just maximizing fixed extrinsic rewards.
